strengths: Clear identification of three specific real-world objects with context (article date/source, memoir nature, algorithmic screenshot).
Explicit and consistent links to the IA prompt on objectivity, foregrounding knowledge acquisition across all objects.
Distinct and coherent justification for each objectâs contribution, supported by theoretical references (Locke, confirmation bias, echo chambers).
weaknesses: Depth and precision in analysis are uneven; some explanations lapse into general statements and lack focus on knowledge production.
References and footnote formatting are inconsistent, with occasional disconnected or incomplete citations.
Discussion sometimes omits explicit TOK terminology (ways of knowing, knowledge frameworks) and could better synthesize insights across objects.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission clearly meets the descriptors for band 7â8: three specific objects are well contextualized and linked to the chosen IA prompt, and each objectâs contribution is justified with appropriate evidence. However, the treatment lacks the consistent depth, rigorous referencing and sophisticated TOK terminology needed to reach the 9â10 band. The uneven analytical precision and formatting issues constrain the overall rigour, making a final score of 7 most appropriate.

annotations.comment: Introduce âalgorithmic biasâ when explaining how tailored feeds shape knowledge acquisition to deepen the TOK analysis.
Good use of Oxfordâs echo chamber definition, but include an in-text citation with author and year to adhere to academic conventions.
Reference to echo-chamber is apt but could be strengthened by a TOK perspective on knowledge communities and shared paradigms.
The title explicitly references the prompt, providing clear focus. Consider briefly defining both âproductionâ and âacquisitionâ of knowledge to set precise TOK scope.
The definition of objectivity is appropriate, but the footnote formatting is inconsistent. Ensure citations follow one style and support the TOK framing.
The header for Object 1 clearly identifies the chosen article and aligns with the structure requirement. Consider adding a brief note on its specific TOK significance.
The image of the article lacks a caption describing its content and context. Add details (date, headline) to clarify its relevance to objectivity.
The discussion of the IB IA criteria is descriptive but doesnât yet link explicitly to TOK concepts of bias or knowledge frameworks. Strengthen the TOK connection.
The theory of Lockeâs empiricism is introduced but the explanation cuts off mid-argument. Complete the link between empirical data and objectivity in acquisition.
Footnote formatting varies. Combine the dictionary entry and access date into one citation, and ensure consistency across all references.
This paragraph reiterates the importance of bias awareness but remains generic. Focus instead on how empirical methods in economics illustrate TOK acquisition challenges.
The global implications paragraph extends the discussion but lacks explicit TOK terminology (e.g., âways of knowingâ). Integrate such terms to ground analysis in TOK.
The header for Object 2 is precise, naming the memoir and author, which strengthens context clarity.
The image lacks a descriptive caption indicating the memoirâs cover or excerpt context. Add a caption to link the visual to the TOK discussion.
Good context for the memoirâs narrative frame, but define âframe narrativeâ and explain how it influences the acquisition of knowledge specifically.
The exploration of perspective bias is relevant, but the connection between authorial bias and the studentâs own acquisition requires deeper TOK analysis.
Footnote for the memoir is properly formatted and provides a credible source. Consider adding page numbers for specific references.
The transition sentence is too brief. Merge it with the next paragraph or expand to connect ideas on bias more cohesively.
The claim that art and media are primary channels is generic. Provide concrete examples or reference studies to strengthen the TOK production of knowledge argument.
The header for Object 3 is clear and labels the social media screenshot well; this aligns with criteria for specific real-world context.
The screenshot lacks metadata (date, user) to ground its real-world context. Include such details to illustrate its relevance to acquisition processes.
This conclusion effectively states objectivity is impossible in online acquisition but reiterates prior points. Consider synthesizing insights across all objects.
The summary of Object 3 ends strongly but lacks an explicit link back to the key prompt distinction between production and acquisition.
The âOther Referencesâ list omits key TOK theorists cited in the text. Add entries for Locke, confirmation bias studies, and echo-chamber literature.
The web source on ways of acquiring knowledge is interesting but non-academic. Seek peer-reviewed journals to support theoretical claims.
The reference format for Jhangiani and Price lacks consistency (missing year, italics). Correct it to meet academic standards.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
